crime.value: Suspected murder disguised as suicide by drugging
crime.quote: 'I think Liza was drugged.' 'I think it was murder.'

victim.value: Liza, a teenager who had a secret daughter and killed herself at a secluded alcove
victim.quote: the place where a teenager sought her final resting place months before

investigator.value: Sarah, the victim's best friend who receives new information about the death
investigator.quote: She wanted to tuck her phone away and ignore him, he deserved it. Frustrated, curiosity overcame her

suspect_architecture.value: Two primary suspects: Mark (romantic connection with motive and guilt) and potentially the mother (control/restriction issues)
suspect_architecture.quote: Rage clouded her mind. She used to think of Mark as her brother. After all, he put her best friend through...

red_herring.value: Suicide assumption deliberately established to misdirect from murder theory

clue_fairness.value: Text message conversation and location details (lily pad incident, tree wishes) available to reader simultaneously
clue_fairness.quote: When she texted me to ask about the 'lily pad' incident, she was probably staring out onto the lake

social_world.value: Parental control dynamics and teenage social restrictions affecting communication and intervention
social_world.quote: If her mum hadn't harped on about her 'needing to socialise' with your cousins

reveal_mechanic.value: Drug evidence as physical proof to demonstrate murder over suicide

media_and_audience.value: Death publicly perceived and accepted as teenage suicide
media_and_audience.quote: the place where a teenager sought her final resting place months before

investigator_wound.value: Survivor's guilt and sense of preventable loss through failed communication
investigator_wound.quote: if her Mum hadn't taken her phone, could she have prevented this?

_meta.title: ebook great a footprint made of code
